chronology.governance_interpretation: The complete root-cause JSON is post-preregistration corroboration only. The preregistered recovery must stand solely on the pre-freeze open-only diagnostic, the unchanged pre-existing 1.0 A technical-validity threshold, and the frozen rule. Any interim observation mentioned in the preregistration does not retroactively make the later complete artifact a frozen source binding.

claim_boundary: This receipt reviews preregistration coherence only. It does not approve an implementation, authorize remote execution or release, unseal test32, reinterpret V4-D as PASS, or establish Docking Gold, binding, affinity, competition, or experimental blocking.

independent_count_and_hash_checks.complete_pair_count_interpretation: If implementation proves every one of the 14,490 native-scored model poses has exactly one 8X6B and one 9E6Y row, the retained complete-pair total must be 14,391. This review does not infer that global closure from the native-only diagnostic alone.

mandatory_implementation_order: Verify exact preregistration, clarification, diagnostic, run-receipt, source-manifest, evaluator, helper, failure-receipt, and generic-prior hashes before any raw path construction.
Validate the full 290-row split and counts, then select exactly OPEN_TRAIN 226 plus OPEN_DEVELOPMENT 32 before constructing any results path; sealed test32 paths must never be constructed.
Select exactly 1548 open jobs with complete candidate by two-conformation by three-seed closure, then partition exactly 1547 raw-success jobs and the one frozen terminal fallback.

Read raw job results as immutable regular-file snapshots, verify job identity, job hash, protocol binding, and recompute the ordered raw-result SHA chain 202d06e1ee689167427abdebcb85705e20ee867aacda31bef5211a35d44d9301.
Construct unique per-job per-model two-reference pairs and require exactly one 8X6B and one 9E6Y score before applying the validity rule.

Read native overlay RMSD from the job-manifest conformation; recompute all values greater than 1.0 and require exactly 99 unique job_id/model keys and canonical SHA256 941d5010190c6576ee0961681227d5b9b1ce9a719cc63ae5c25a45b0f8de9c1f.
Drop both reference rows for each invalid job_id/model pair before classification, sorting, weighting, utility, or aggregation; do not drop any additional pair.

Require the exact 99 invalid keys to correspond to complete two-reference model pairs; after filtering, require exactly 98 affected jobs and 83 affected candidates and at least four retained complete pairs for every successful job. Independently prove global two-reference closure; only if all 14,490 native-scored model poses are complete is the exact retained total 14,391. The affected-job retained minimum must reproduce as five.
Sort retained pairs only by native_haddock_score then model, recompute and renormalize rank weights, and compute model-count reliability from the filtered pair count.

Apply the unchanged V1 teacher formulas and require at least two successful seeds per candidate per conformation; emit exactly 258 finite teacher rows with split counts 226 and 32 and primary target R_dual_min.
Use the single terminal fallback only for the frozen failed job, parse zero aggregate metric fields, permit no second fallback, and preserve explicit missing-seed uncertainty.
Create release artifacts only in the new V1.2 root with exclusive or atomic publication after all gates pass; never modify V1 or V1.1 roots, statuses, receipts, or failed release absence.
Emit all preregistered descriptive sensitivity reports and the explicit development-only, source-evaluator-FAIL, no-formal-unlock claim boundary.
mandatory_test_and_second_review_expecta: Positive real-fixture replay must prove exact counts, hashes, filtered pair set, retained ordering, finite 258-row output, and deterministic replay.
Mutating any invalid pair identity or value, adding/removing an invalid pair, changing threshold comparison, or dropping only one reference must fail closed.
A valid-pose metric mutation that preserves the invalid list must still fail through the frozen raw-result SHA-chain binding.
Cross-reference missing/duplicate rows, duplicate model keys, non-finite RMSD, wrong native reference, fewer than four retained complete pairs, or a second terminal failure must fail closed.

Tests must prove filtering occurs before classification and native-HADDOCK sorting, and that rank weights and model-count reliability are recomputed from retained pairs rather than inherited from pre-filter ranks.
Symlink, non-regular file, same-tick/TOCTOU mutation, output preexistence, and partial-publication scenarios must fail without publishing a release.
Instrumented access tests must prove test32 raw path constructions, file opens, metric reads, and label rows are all zero and that the results root is never listed or globbed.

The builder must not consume the post-preregistration root-cause artifact for filtering, target computation, or release authorization; it may only be carried in a separately marked corroborative report.
The post-freeze canonicalization clarification must be accepted only by independently reproducing the preregistered canonical SHA; its self-declared no-method-change flags are not sufficient alone.
The second reviewer must verify V1 and V1.1 failure receipt hashes/statuses and absence of old releases, and must separately approve implementation bytes before any launch freeze is created.

Do not equate native-metric pose count with complete two-reference pair count until the builder has independently enforced exact reference cardinality for every model; preserve the original formula helper behavior for any non-invalid incomplete pair unless the frozen contract explicitly rejects it.
review_scope: Independent read-only review of the frozen V1.2 preregistration, post-freeze canonicalization clarification, open-only overlay diagnostic v2, post-preregistration root-cause diagnostic, and immutable V1/V1.1 failure receipts.

canonicalization_clarification.authority: post-freeze serialization clarification only; no threshold or scientific method authority

root_cause_diagnostic.authority: corroborative only; complete artifact postdates preregistration and may not authorize or define the V1.2 method

limitations_requiring_disclosure: Although only 0.683 percent of native pose pairs are invalid, 83 of 258 open candidates are exposed; this is broad at candidate level and requires the frozen descriptive sensitivity reports.
The invalidity is strongly conformation-asymmetric (89 8X6B versus 10 9E6Y), so distributional shifts in R_8X6B and R_dual_min must be reported.

The root-cause artifact supports the huge 8X6B class with high confidence but does not explain all 99 pairs, especially the mild 9E6Y rows; the uniform technical gate, not a universal root-cause claim, is the method justification.
This is a post-hoc development-only teacher recovery and cannot repair the formal V4-D FAIL or support formal test claims.
reasons: The 1.0 A native-receptor overlay criterion is the unchanged pre-existing hard technical-validity gate; it is not a relaxed or candidate-specific threshold.
The criterion uses native-receptor structural integrity rather than a desired hotspot, occlusion, tier, or target value, reducing direct outcome-based selection.
Dropping the entire two-reference model pair prevents one-reference cherry-picking and preserves paired 8X6B/9E6Y comparison semantics.

Filtering before geometry classification, HADDOCK-score sorting, rank-weight normalization, utility calculation, and candidate aggregation prevents invalid pairs from consuming rank mass; weights must then be renormalized over retained pairs.
The diagnostic indicates sparse pair-level invalidity and retained coverage above the frozen minimum in every affected job.

supersession.reason: Correct terminology: the open diagnostic directly proves 14,490 model poses with one native overlay metric, not global two-reference completeness. Complete-pair closure remains an implementation/review gate. No preregistration decision, threshold, invalid identity, or scientific method changed.
